mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0% and remains elevated for two consecutive months, the restrictive monetary stance compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates. This triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days as market participants reprice growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:current FEDFUNDS at 5.33% indicates restrictive cycle
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with elevated rates
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market momentum that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings. This always-on signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market prices in sustained economic expansion.
evidence_refs: fact:current PAYEMS at 158,133 thousand indicates strong labor market
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of months when PAYEMS growth is positive, or correlation between PAYEMS momentum and forward returns falls below 0.10 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales (RSAFS) decline month-over-month while the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises simultaneously, this joint signal indicates weakening consumer demand and labor market deterioration. The mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as earnings expectations compress.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines MoM AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in same month
evidence_refs: fact:current RSAFS at 709,590M and UNRATE at 3.8%
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over 5-year training window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines below 102.0 index level, signaling supply-side weakness and reduced manufacturing capacity utilization. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as the market reprices profit margins downward due to supply constraints.
evidence_refs: fact:current INDPRO at 102.3313 near trigger threshold
regime_hint: supply-constrained environment with elevated input costs
failure_condition: INDPRO < 102.0 produces negative 5-day returns in fewer than 52% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) both accelerate month-over-month simultaneously, indicating broad-based demand expansion across residential and consumer sectors. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market prices in sustained economic growth.
candidate_trigger: HOUST MoM growth > 2% AND RSAFS MoM growth > 1% in same month
evidence_refs: fact:current HOUST at 1,521K and RSAFS at 709,590M
regime_hint: early-to-mid cycle expansion with synchronized demand growth
failure_condition: Joint signal produces positive 60-day returns in fewer than 58% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in linear regression over 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises above 315 index level while the federal funds rate remains below 4.5%, real rates turn negative, eroding cash returns and driving capital into equities. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days in negative real rate environments.
evidence_refs: fact:current CPIAUCSL at 312.23 and FEDFUNDS at 5.33%
regime_hint: negative real rate regime with inflation above trend
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 56% of instances when real rates are negative, or the interaction term is not significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 4.0% for two consecutive months, signaling labor market deterioration and reduced consumer confidence. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations decline due to weakening demand.
evidence_refs: fact:current UNRATE at 3.8% remains below trigger
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: UNRATE > 4.0% produces negative 60-day returns in fewer than 54% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarter-over-quarter remains positive and above 2.0% annualized rate, signaling sustained economic expansion. This always-on trend signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market prices in continued earnings growth from expanding economic activity.
evidence_refs: fact:current GDPC1 at 22,679.255B indicates ongoing expansion
regime_hint: any regime with positive GDP growth
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 48% of quarters with positive GDPC1 growth, or correlation between GDPC1 growth rate and forward returns falls below 0.12 over any 3-year window.
